description_long: This is a small, single-story cabin with a simple rectangular footprint and a dark brown gable roof. The exterior walls are light-colored, likely painted wood or siding, and the roof has a textured, shingle-like appearance. There are several small, symmetrically placed windows on the longer sides, each with a grid pattern, providing natural light to the interior. The cabin sits on a flat, circular base, suggesting it is meant to be free-standing and not attached to other structures. No porch or veranda is visible, and the entrance door is not clearly shown in any of the views. The overall design is modest and functional, typical of a compact residential cabin found in suburban or park settings.
description: A small, light-colored rectangular cabin with a dark gable roof and symmetrically placed grid windows, set on a flat base.
description_view_0: The cabin is seen from the front, showing the long side with two small grid windows and the dark gable roof.
description_view_1: The cabin is viewed from a corner, highlighting the gable end and one side wall with a window visible.
description_view_2: The rear long side of the cabin is visible, mirroring the front with two small grid windows and the same roof style.
description_view_3: The opposite gable end is shown, with a plain wall and no visible windows or doors.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with moderate pitch, covered in dark shingles
attribute_window_arrangement: Four small, rectangular windows symmetrically placed in pairs on each long side, each with a grid pattern; no windows on the gable ends
attribute_door_placement: No exterior doors are visible in the provided views
